title: Cypher parser: full openCypher surface before the query.Query freeze
description: Umbrella for the pre-freeze scope expansion (owner decision 2026-07-01). gqlc generates type-safe repository implementations for practically any openCypher query, including writes, so the freeze (gqlc-cta) must wait for: expression core + typing (Stage 6), temporal (7), full patterns (8), read-clause completion (9), aggregations (10), predicates (11), writes (12), MERGE (13), CALL + procedure registry (14), and the TCK corpus sweep. Sibling of gqlc-ude (read surface, Stages 1-5, complete). gqlc-cta dependencies extended to each piece.

title: Stage 14: CALL + procedure signature registry
description: New compile-time input artifact: procedure signatures, declared alongside the schema (TCK precedent step: there exists a procedure test.my.proc(in :: INTEGER?) :: (out :: INTEGER?)). Type CALL...YIELD, YIELD *, and standalone CALL - the latter two are untypeable without the signature, so an unreferenced/unknown procedure is a generation-time compile error (a feature: fails at codegen instead of runtime). Needs godog step defs that build the per-scenario registry. Wire TCK dir: clauses/call (~41 scenario outlines).

title: Stage 13: MERGE + ON CREATE / ON MATCH
description: MERGE upsert semantics on top of the Stage 12 write model: match-or-create pattern binding plus conditional SET branches (ON CREATE SET / ON MATCH SET). Both branches contribute to parameter typing. Wire TCK dir: clauses/merge (~75 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 12.

title: Stage 12: write clauses - CREATE, DELETE, SET, REMOVE
description: The headline scope expansion: query.Query gains a read/write statement distinction (drivers need transaction mode), a write-effect shape, and projection-less statements (no RETURN). SET value typing against the schema yields parameter type inference for writes - the core of the repository-pattern goal (typed Create/Update/Delete methods). DETACH DELETE included. TCK side-effect assertion steps (the side effects should be) are reinterpreted parse-level per gqlc-39s. Wire TCK dirs: clauses/{create,delete,set,remove} (~205 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6 (SET/CREATE property expressions).

title: Stage 11: predicate expressions - quantifiers + existential subqueries
description: ALL/ANY/NONE/SINGLE list quantifiers and EXISTS {} existential subqueries (patterns inside expressions - scoping rules matter: inner pattern variables must not leak into the outer scope). Result type is always BOOLEAN so model impact is thin; grammar and scoping are the work. Wire TCK dirs: expressions/{quantifier,existentialSubqueries} (~110 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6.

title: Stage 10: aggregations
description: count/sum/avg/min/max/collect + DISTINCT: detect aggregates in projections, model implicit grouping keys and the cardinality implications for generated row types. Parser-side counterpart of gqlc-gyw (resolver grouping-key semantics) - coordinate the model shape with that bead. Wire TCK dir: expressions/aggregation (~27 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6 expression typing.
close_reason: Stage 10 aggregations merged as PR #62 (2e9f427), GH #45 closed. Round-1 review PASS (first of run); docs-only fix round (SF-1..4: skiplist comment, spec wire-truth amendments §1.2/§1.4/§8). Suite 2886/2468/418/0 → 2921/2496/425/0; pins 66 mustParse / 12 mustReject; sentinels 4. Follow-ups filed: gqlc-33k.1 (aggregate-kind preservation), gqlc-33k.2 (projection-list DISTINCT).

title: Stage 9: read-clause completion - ORDER BY, WITH...WHERE, UNWIND
description: Close out the read-clause surface: ORDER BY on RETURN and WITH (ordering does not change row types but its expressions must be accepted and validated), WITH...WHERE, WITH skip/limit, and UNWIND (introduces a new binding whose element type derives from the list expression - needs Stage 6 typing). Wire TCK dirs: clauses/{return-orderby,with-orderBy,with-skip-limit,with-where,unwind} (~176 scenario outlines).
close_reason: Stage 9 landed: PR #60 squash-merged as 9c39a50, GH issue #46 auto-closed. Two review rounds: round-1 caught B1 UnwindBinding collision matrix holes + B2 unconditional pattern-reuse skip; fixed via symmetric kind-conflict checks + elemType gate. Suite 2886/2468/418/0, pins 59/12.

title: Stage 7: temporal types
description: Add DATE/TIME/LOCALTIME/DATETIME/LOCALDATETIME/DURATION to the type vocabulary; accept and type temporal literals, accessors, and arithmetic (semantics pass through to the driver per ADR 0005 - only result types matter). Wire TCK dir: expressions/temporal (~89 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6 expression typing.
close_reason: Stage 7 temporal types merged via PR #56 (squash f3589e9); review PASS after one fix round (operator-aware promotion); suite 2399/1941/458/0; GH issue #47 closed

title: Stage 8: full pattern surface - named paths, variable-length, multi-type relationships
description: Reverses the largest deliberate read-core exclusions (permanent ErrUnsupportedPattern): named paths (161 pending), variable-length relationships (59), multi-type relationships (2). Adds PATH to the type vocabulary; path variables become projectable bindings. Wire TCK dirs: expressions/{path,pattern,graph}. Also revisit the Stage-5 undirected model for var-length interaction.
close_reason: Stage 8 landed: PR #58 squash-merged as 53d20ea, GH issue #48 auto-closed. Adversarial review round 2 PASS (round 1 caught B1 synthetic-name collision — fixed via PathMember tagged sum). Suite 2517/2068/449/0, pins 52/6.

title: Stage 6: expression core - full scalar expression surface + result typing
description: Grammar-complete scalar expressions and compute result types for projections; retire the permanent ErrUnsupportedProjection category (53 pending scenarios of rich RETURN expressions). Completes the base type vocabulary (list, map, primitive coercions) - the type set is part of the interface to be frozen. Foundational: writes (SET values), aggregation, temporal, and predicates all build on this. Wire TCK dirs: expressions/{literals,boolean,comparison,mathematical,string,null,precedence,typeConversion,list,map,conditional}. Runtime-error scenarios in those dirs go to the documented out-of-scope skiplist category (see gqlc-39s).
close_reason: Stage 6 rich expressions merged via PR #54 (squash eb9db79); review PASS; suite 1395/955/440/0; GH issue #49 closed

title: ADR: expand pre-freeze scope to full openCypher surface (writes, CALL, expressions)
description: Product goal (owner, 2026-07-01): gqlc must generate type-safe, compile-safe repository implementations for practically ANY openCypher query - reads AND writes. ADR 0004 planned Stages 1-5 (read core) then freeze; that is now insufficient: query.Query must also model writes, MERGE, CALL, full patterns, and the full expression/type surface BEFORE the freeze (gqlc-cta), because retrofitting them after codegen exists would be far more expensive. Write a new ADR documenting: (a) the three TCK buckets - must-model (writes/merge/call/patterns/read clauses), must-parse-with-thin-model-impact (most expressions: only the result type matters, semantics pass through per ADR 0005), out-of-scope (runtime-error scenarios; result/side-effect assertions are reinterpreted at parse level); (b) explicit reversal of the permanent read-core skiplist exclusions (named paths x161, variable-length x59, multi-type x2, rich projections x53, writes x9) - those were deliberate Stage-0-5 scope cuts, now in scope; (c) procedure signatures as a new compile-time input artifact alongside the schema (TCK precedent: 'there exists a procedure test.my.proc(in :: INTEGER?) :: (out :: INTEGER?)'); (d) stage renumbering 6-14 with per-stage specs in docs/specs/ as before. Type vocabulary completeness (lists, maps, temporal, path) is part of the frozen interface and must be settled here.

title: CI: golangci-lint + GitHub Actions + git hooks + branch protection
description: Add static analysis and CI: .golangci.yml (v2, curated linter set incl. exhaustive/depguard/musttag), one GitHub Actions workflow with parallel jobs (lint/test/tidy), path-filtered vuln+actionlint workflows, just recipes as single command surface, .githooks (pre-commit: block-master+fmt+vet; pre-push: test+diff-lint) folding in the add-git-guardrails branch, and master branch protection with required checks. Agreed in session 2026-07-01: no grammar-sync job, no -race, golangci-lint binary pinned in justfile, CI runs just recipes with explicit caching instead of golangci-lint-action.
close_reason: PR opened: golangci config + fixes, CI workflows, hooks, branch protection. Closes via merge.

title: Resolver / codegen / generated driver
description: The downstream consumers ADR 0004 reserves for post-freeze: the schema-aware resolver, the type-safe code generator, and the generated driver wiring. Each is written once against the stable frozen model. Per ADR 0004 §Decision.

title: Freeze query.Query + revise ADR 0003 + CONTEXT.md
description: When Stages 1-5 are complete, lock the query model. Revise ADR 0003 to declare the model frozen and document its final shape. Update CONTEXT.md glossary to the final terminology. Also specify the resolver's API shape in the freeze ADR: schema-bound constructor (`resolver.New(schema).Resolve(query)`) per the deferral agreed during Stage 2 grilling — see ADR 0006 §Consequences ("Resolver API shape is deferred"). ValidatedQuery lives in the resolver package. This is the gate ADR 0004 names: no consumer is built until this lands.
notes: Pre-freeze items surfaced by the static-analysis PR (gqlc-nmg, 2026-07-01): (1) RESOLVED 2026-07-02 by PR #38 — query.QueryBranch/QueryPart renamed to query.Branch/query.Part (matches CONTEXT.md glossary); both nolint:revive markers removed. Decision taken by owner ahead of the freeze. (2) Parameter's JSON keys were fixed to lowercase name/uses (musttag finding) — a wire change already taken pre-freeze; goldens rebaselined. (3) Post-freeze follow-up: add an apidiff CI gate on internal/query so the frozen API is mechanically enforced.

title: Stage 1 — non-property-typed parameters (SKIP/LIMIT $p)
description: First model evolution after Stage 0: parameters whose type comes from their slot rather than a binding property. Specifically bare $p in SKIP and LIMIT. Introduces query.Use as a sealed sum and relaxes invariant D4.
design:
Unlock bare SKIP $p / LIMIT $p as non-property-typed parameter uses.

Model change:
- Introduce sealed sum `query.Use` = PropertyUse | PositionalUse (mirrors Binding/Endpoint discipline)
- PropertyUse wraps a Ref (existing case)
- PositionalUse carries a `Position` enum (PositionSkip | PositionLimit), int-backed, stringer (mirrors graph.EntityKind)
- `Parameter.Uses` type changes from []Ref to []Use
- Invariant D4 relaxes: every Use is exactly one of PropertyUse | PositionalUse
- Smart constructors enforce invariants
- Tagged-union MarshalJSON with lowercase "kind": PropertyUse flattens to {"kind":"property","variable":...,"property":...}; PositionalUse to {"kind":"positional","position":"skip"|"limit"}

Accept/reject delta from Stage 0:
- SKIP $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Skip}
- LIMIT $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Limit}
- SKIP $p + 1, LIMIT f($p), ORDER BY $p, every other deferred-parameter construct: stay rejected
- Accept rule mirrors WHERE D1 directness discipline: bare positional only

Dedup unchanged: Parameter dedup by Name in first-appearance order. Mixed-kind uses on one Parameter are fine; the resolver judges type unification post-freeze (parser stays schema-agnostic per ADR 0003).

JSON casing wart: Parameter struct stays uppercase Go-default while Uses items go lowercase tagged-union. Acknowledged scope-creep-avoidance — defer harmonization to post-freeze.
acceptance_criteria:
- TCK scenarios in clauses/return-skip-limit/ exercising SKIP $p / LIMIT $p flip red→green
- Layer 2 targeted sentinel tests cover still-rejected cases (SKIP $p+1, ORDER BY $p)
- No previously-passing scenario regresses; pending count only decreases
- Both gated-swarm reviewers (Linus + Sandi) approve the diff
- `just test` green
notes:
Plan agreed via /grill-with-docs on 2026-05-26.

Reference docs:
- ADR 0004 — test-first, evolving-until-feature-complete (governs staged approach)
- ADR 0003 — schema-agnostic parser; resolver handles type unification
- ADR 0005 — generated code executes original text; model is type-interface only
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-0.md — Stage 0 spec (canonical baseline)

Deliverables in the same PR as the code:
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md — short delta-style spec doc (~80-120 lines); structure mirrors Stage 0
- CONTEXT.md gains a "Use" entry; "Parameter" refined to reference it
- Stage 0 spec §4 C2 erratum: one-line note clarifying that label-conflict against the schema is the resolver's responsibility (the conjunctive constraint interpretation is faithful to openCypher, not a parser limitation)

TCK dir widening: add clauses/return-skip-limit/ to the godog dir list. Expect 1-2 skiplist additions to surface during implementation (runtime-error assertions we cannot model).

Implementation methodology: /tdd inside /gated-swarm (Carmack implements; Linus + Sandi review).
close_reason: Stage 1 feature-complete: model evolution (Use sum + ClauseSlotUse), parser change (mineClauseSlotParameter for SKIP+LIMIT bare $p), TCK corpus widening (return-skip-limit/ + 18-entry skiplist + TestSkiplistOrphans), Layer-2 rule update (directional rule + AUTHORED pin for non-bare $p in SKIP/LIMIT), documentation bundle (Stage 1 spec + CONTEXT.md Use entry + Stage 0 §4 C2 note). All five cycles approved by both gated-swarm reviewers (Linus + Metz). just test green at 509/315/0/194.

title: Cypher query.Parser: feature-complete the read surface
description: Umbrella for the staged build-out of internal/query/cypher. Per ADR 0004: evolve query.Query through Stages 1-5 (test-first against the openCypher TCK), then lock the model and build the resolver / codegen / generated driver. No consumer is built until after the freeze.

title: Model projection-list DISTINCT (RETURN DISTINCT / WITH DISTINCT) as Part-level axis
description: Reviewer S-1 from Stage 10 (PR for gqlc-2av): MATCH (n) RETURN DISTINCT n lowers to the same model as MATCH (n) RETURN n — clause-level DISTINCT silently dropped. Stage 10's rationale for modelling aggregate-DISTINCT (cardinality-changing single-bit axis; two observably-different queries must not lower to indistinguishable models) applies verbatim. Fix direction: Part.Distinct bool with always-emit JSON, mirroring AggregateProjection.Distinct.

title: Preserve AggregateFunc kind for aggregates over rich expressions
description: Reviewer SF-3 from Stage 10 (PR for gqlc-2av): when an aggregate's argument is not a bare var/literal/star (sum(x+1), count(n.age+1), collect(a OR b), count(count(*)), min([1,2,3]), sum(range(1,3))), classifyProjection falls through to ExprProjection and the AggregateFunc kind is silently dropped — resolver cannot distinguish an aggregate column from a plain expression of the same type. Inherited from Stage 6; Stage 10's DISTINCT rationale (two observably-different queries must not lower to indistinguishable models) applies verbatim. Fix direction: classifyFunction recognizes aggregate invocations with expression arguments and keeps the outer kind, or a dedicated axis on ExprProjection. Spec stage-10 §8 documents the gap.

title: Reject projection-position pattern predicates (Pattern1 [22]/[23])
description: Stage 8 review SF3: patterns used as scalar expressions in RETURN/WITH projection position (Pattern1 [22]/[23], TCK SyntaxError:UnexpectedSyntax) currently parse-accept and sit as enumerated skiplist entries in acceptance_test.go. Per ADR 0007 this is bucket-1 (context-sensitive parse rule): pattern predicates are legal only in WHERE/EXISTS positions. Detect the position-specific misuse and reject (likely alongside Stage 11 predicate work, which touches pattern-in-expression contexts). Remove the two skiplist entries when done. Parent epic gqlc-33k.

title: Grammar: shortestPath()/allShortestPaths() not parseable — ANTLR grammar gap
description: Stage 8 review finding: MATCH p = shortestPath((a)-[*]->(b)) hits an ANTLR 'no viable alternative' parse error — the vendored openCypher grammar (internal/grammar/cypher) appears not to implement shortestPath/allShortestPaths, though openCypher considers them valid. Below the model layer entirely. Needed before the freeze claim of full pattern support; likely grammar regeneration or upstream grammar version bump, then model decision (path-typed function-like pattern). Parent epic gqlc-33k.

title: TCK corpus completion: wire remaining dirs + codify out-of-scope skiplist policy
description: Final sweep after Stages 6-14: every TCK feature dir must be either wired into the godog suite or documented out-of-scope. Wire useCases/{countingSubgraphMatches,triadicSelection} as parse corpus. Give skiplist entries named policy categories (runtime-error, result-assertion-only) so pending unambiguously means work remaining and the suite is a true progress meter. Acceptance: zero unwired dirs, zero unexplained skiplist entries, godog green.

title: Gate merges on actionlint (always) and govulncheck (on dep changes)
description: lint/test/tidy are required checks; actionlint and vuln are advisory. Make actionlint an always-run required job in ci.yml, and make govulncheck required via the no-op twin pattern (same-named job on inverse path filter) so it blocks exactly the PRs that touch go.mod/go.sum.
close_reason: actionlint always-run required job in ci.yml; govulncheck required via single conditional workflow (in-job dep-diff, not the twin pattern)

title: Release automation: release-please (tag per release, no binaries)
description: Set up automated release tagging on master. Decision (2026-07-01): tag per RELEASE, not per merge — release-please-action maintains a rolling Release PR on pushes to master; merging it creates the semver tag + GitHub Release + CHANGELOG. Version bumps computed from Conventional Commit PR titles (squash-merge via Graphite means master commits = PR titles, so shift titles to feat:/fix:/etc). DESCOPED (2026-07-01): GoReleaser/binaries — main.go is a dev scratch harness with no real CLI surface, and user decided we likely never ship binaries; consumers use go install / go get. Releases = tag + version + GitHub Release with changelog (layers 1-3). If a real CLI ever emerges and binaries are wanted, file a new bead.
design: 1) .github/workflows/release-please.yml on push to master, token fallback: secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN || GITHUB_TOKEN (PAT needed once required status checks exist, else Release PR gets no CI runs). 2) release-please-config.json: release-type=go, bump-minor-pre-major=true (feat!: bumps minor while 0.x). 3) .release-please-manifest.json seeded at 0.0.0 — first Release PR should propose 0.1.0; if bootstrap proposes something odd, force with release-as then remove. 4) .github/CODEOWNERS per notes. 5) Tag ruleset (pattern v*) in GitHub UI restricting creation. 6) No just recipe: release-please is GitHub-side API automation, not a check — nothing to run locally, so CI-parity rule doesn't apply.
acceptance_criteria: Merging PRs to master updates a rolling Release PR; merging the Release PR creates v* tag + GitHub Release with changelog; no binary artifacts; manual tag pushes blocked by tag ruleset; CODEOWNERS present.
notes:
CODEOWNERS (do alongside this work, prep for future maintainers): add .github/CODEOWNERS with a catch-all line plus explicit entries pinning release/CI pipeline paths to the owner — these paths can mint releases / run arbitrary CI code, so they must keep owner review even after source dirs are delegated. Example (last matching pattern wins):
*                       @antranig-yeretzian
/.github/               @antranig-yeretzian
/.goreleaser.yaml       @antranig-yeretzian
/release-please-config.json @antranig-yeretzian
While solo the catch-all makes the rest redundant; value is documenting intent for when e.g. /internal/query/ gets a maintainer. Interaction: release-please's Release PR touches CHANGELOG.md + manifest, so once 'Require review from Code Owners' is enabled (maintainer-#2 day), the owner must approve every Release PR — acceptable/desirable: releases become an explicit owner decision. Patterns for not-yet-existing files are harmless (no-ops).

title: Rename module path to github.com/areqag/gqlc
description: GitHub username changed from antranig-yeretzian to areqag. Update go.mod module directive and all import paths (49 refs across 24 .go files). Leave .beads/*.jsonl historical records untouched.
close_reason: Module path renamed to github.com/areqag/gqlc; PR #31 opened, thermo-nuclear review passed, just test green

title: Resolver: undirected edge two-orientation trial + multi-match semantics
description: Post-freeze resolver contract handed off by Stage 5 (gqlc-7z6). An EdgeBinding with directed=false has NO authoritative orientation: the parser records endpoints in textual order only. The resolver MUST form TWO candidate schema.EdgeKeys (source->target AND target->source) and accept the binding if EITHER resolves against the directed-only schema. OPEN QUESTION (spec cypher-query-parser-stage-5.md §9): the trial can match zero, one, or TWO distinct directed edge types (schema has both A->B and B->A with the queried label) — define whether a double match is ambiguous, a union, or an error, and whether the resolver must report WHICH orientation(s) matched (which may require widening the marker bool->enum, cheap pre-consumer). Lives downstream of resolver bead gqlc-0mx and the freeze gqlc-cta.

title: Stage 3 — projections & aggregations
description: Extend RETURN beyond bare-variable / single-property: expressions, aggregates (count/sum/collect/...), literals, function calls, RETURN *. ReturnItem becomes an expression sum. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
notes: Stage 3 on branch stage-3-projections (single branch, separated commits). DONE: (1) grill-with-docs -> spec docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md committed (d652c11). (2) MODEL commit f053af8 PUSHED: ReturnItem.Value Projection sealed sum + AggregateFunc enum + Query.ReturnsAll + JSON + 77 goldens; Linus gate PASS; pure refactor (180 pending unchanged), just test green. NEXT sub-step = UNLOCK commit: listener classifies projections (Ref/Literal/Func/Aggregate) + accepts RETURN * (set Query.ReturnsAll), add skiplist group per spec §6 (NoVariablesInScope/UnknownFunction/ColumnNameConflict), Layer-2 swaps (+2 mustParse, -1 return-star mustReject, +1 authored residual ErrUnsupportedProjection), trim ErrUnsupportedProjection docstring, regen new goldens. Use /tdd red then /gated-swarm Carmack+Linus. Spec is source of truth. Verify just test green + pending < 180 before close.
close_reason: Stage 3 landed on branch stage-3-projections. Model commit f053af8 (Projection sealed sum + AggregateFunc + Query.ReturnsAll, Linus gate PASS, pure refactor). Unlock commit (this): listener classifies var/var.prop/scalar-literal/func/aggregate + count(*) + RETURN *->ReturnsAll; 3 skiplist entries (NoVariablesInScope/UnknownFunction/ColumnNameConflict); +2 mustParse, -1 return-star mustReject, +1 authored residual; errors docstring trimmed; 10 new goldens; CONTEXT.md Projection/Aggregate + ADR 0003 note. TDD red->green, Linus gate PASS. godog 329/180 -> 336/173 (+7 passed, -7 pending). just test green. Follow-ups filed: gqlc-gyw (resolver grouping), gqlc-zir (unary-minus nit).

title: Stage 4 — multi-scope & UNION
description: Support WITH chaining (intermediate scopes that project the values Stage 3 introduces) and UNION branches. query.Query gains part/branch structure. Per ADR 0004 §Stages; explicitly depends on Stage 3.
close_reason: Stage 4 done: WITH chaining + UNION. Restructured query.Query into Branches(UNION)->Parts(WITH) with UnionKind enum; per-part scope threading (exported names L-to-R, WITH * transitive, cross-branch isolation); parameters stay query-wide. godog read-core 371 passed / 179 pending (was 336/173 at Stage 3 end; +35 scenarios parse, clauses/with+clauses/union added to readCoreDirs). 7 WITH/UNION negatives skiplisted (all verified below the type-interface boundary by adversarial review). Linus gate APPROVED. Commits 4491fb8(model)+b6cd944(unlock)+5821e4c(prep) pushed to origin/stage-4-with-union. NO PR per loop discipline.

title: Stage 2 — nullability (OPTIONAL MATCH)
description: Make bindings introduced by OPTIONAL MATCH nullable, and propagate that flag through the columns derived from them so codegen can emit pointer (or option) types post-freeze. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
close_reason: Stage 2 complete: nullable bindings, listener accepts OPTIONAL MATCH, Layer-2 + goldens updated. Spec doc at docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-2.md. Stack: stage-2-prep → stage-2-model → stage-2-unlock → stage-2-docs. just test green, godog 329/0/180.

title: Split dev setup out of README.md into CONTRIBUTING.md
description: README.md currently mixes project description with dev/contributor setup instructions (just init, hook explanation, just recipe list). Move the 'Setup after cloning' and 'Development' sections into a new CONTRIBUTING.md, leaving README.md focused on what the project is, with a pointer link to CONTRIBUTING.md for contributors.

title: ErrVariableKindConflict message text stale for path/unwind conflicts
description: errors.go:41 message string is 'variable used as both node and edge', but Stage 9 reuses the sentinel for path-vs-unwind and unwind-vs-unwind kind conflicts (and Stage 8 for path conflicts). errors.Is classification is correct; only the human-readable text misleads. Reword to cover all kind pairs or parameterize with the two kinds. Found in Stage 9 re-review (non-blocking note).

title: Add Layer-2 mustParse pin for WITH-aggregate-WHERE scope snapshot (foaf shape)
description: Reviewer suggestion (Stage 6, non-blocking): With7 [2] scope-leak regression (mineWhere refs snapshot/restore at expr.go) is guarded only by the godog corpus. Add a mustParse pin for 'MATCH (a) WITH a, count(*) AS c WHERE c > 1 RETURN a' to lock the snapshot/restore against future refactors at Layer 2.

title: Narrow bucket-3 whitelist for InvalidNumberLiteral/InvalidUnicode* details
description: Reviewer finding (Stage 6, non-blocking): InvalidNumberLiteral / InvalidUnicodeCharacter / InvalidUnicodeLiteral in the acceptance_test.go bucket-3 detail whitelist cover a mixed set — some TCK scenarios are value-level (out-of-range ints, \uH in lexed string), others parse-shape (0x with no digits, em-dash operator). Safe today (parser rejects the parse-shape subset; 0 fails) but reduces regression sensitivity to a future lexer bug labeled with those details. Narrow to the value-out-of-range subset.

title: Detect dead git-hook activation (core.hooksPath drift)
description: Discovered 2026-07-02: core.hooksPath in .git/config pointed at the empty .git/hooks (absolute path), silently disabling the .githooks pre-commit/pre-push gates for an unknown period — every local commit/push since ran ungated; CI caught nothing because it is the authoritative gate, but the local budget-split design (README) was dead. Origin of the override unknown (bd doctor silent on hooks; global config clean). Fixed by re-running 'just init' and verified via the hook's BRANCH_OVERRIDE test seam. Needs a cheap drift detector that runs where developers already are, since CI cannot see local git config — e.g., a warning in a frequently-run just recipe (test/lint) when core.hooksPath != .githooks, or a 'just doctor' recipe. Keep it sub-second per the hook budget philosophy.

title: Resolver: implicit grouping-key semantics for aggregate projections
description: Stage 3 (gqlc-npg) carries AggregateProjection with only its []Ref payload + the aggregate kind, deliberately NOT modelling implicit grouping (which non-aggregate RETURN columns form the group key). Per spec §9 (cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md) this is a resolver/Stage-4 concern entangled with WITH, and there is a real risk the []Ref-only payload proves insufficient and AggregateProjection must be reopened. This issue tracks adding grouping-key resolution once the model is frozen (gqlc-cta) and WITH lands (Stage 4, gqlc-v23).

title: Resolver: flow-type nullable bindings across clause structure
description:
Demote a binding's effective Nullable flag in the validated query when downstream clauses prove the binding must exist on every surviving row. Two regimes with different model dependencies:

(a) Edge-chain demotion (no new model structure beyond Stage 2 needed) — a non-nullable edge implies its endpoints exist, so chain 'non-nullable edge → non-nullable endpoints → demote those endpoints' nullability'. Handles the common case: OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b)-[:S]->(c) RETURN a, b, c — both a and b demote.

(b) Bare-pattern demotion (requires Stage 4 clause structure) — a required clause that reuses an OPTIONAL-introduced binding as a bare node/edge pattern filters NULL-rows. E.g. OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b) RETURN b. The model needs per-clause structure to record the second reference; Stage 4 (WITH/UNION) introduces that structure.

Per ADR 0006: the parser does not do this; the resolver does. (a) is shippable on its own after the freeze + resolver exists; (b) additionally waits on Stage 4.

title: docs: Stage 1 spec §6 — make 5→3 arithmetic explicit (Metz cycle-5 non-blocking note)
description: In docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md §6 Layer-2 coverage gaps, the opening claim says ErrUnsupportedParameter is reachable by 5 fail-sites and the list shows 3 remaining uncovered. The intermediate isn't shown: 5 = 1 (cycle-introduced AUTHORED pin) + 1 (whole-map, corpus-covered by Match1[6] in mustReject) + 3 (uncovered). A reader counting 5-1=4 vs 3 bullets pauses unnecessarily. Insert one sentence between 'five distinct fail-sites' and 'the remaining three' that names Match1[6]'s coverage explicitly. Metz's exact suggested phrasing is in her cycle-5 diff review.

title: Stage 5 — undirected patterns
description: Support (a)-[r]-(b) patterns matching in either direction. Edge binding gains a direction-agnostic marker; resolution tries both orientations against the directed schema. May end up N/A if the schema stays directed-only — re-evaluate when reached. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
close_reason: Stage 5 complete: undirected relationship patterns (a)-[r]-(b) and (a)<-[r]->(b) now parse. EdgeBinding gains a positive 'directed' marker (field+Directed() accessor+trailing constructor param+always-emitted JSON key); listener uses an inverted zero-value-safe 'undirected' raw field, polarity flip at toBinding. collectEdge stops rejecting left==right; directed edges canonicalise, undirected keep textual order (no flip; <--> guarded). godog read-core 371/179 -> 380/170 (+9 passed). Linus gate APPROVED. Branch stage-5-undirected pushed to origin (commits dae7fb3 prep, b474fbe model, d4fbedd unlock). Not N/A: parser is schema-agnostic, marker is load-bearing (undirected yields no single EdgeKey, resolver forms two candidate keys).

title: Parser: unary-minus over a projection (RETURN -n.num) accepts as a plain ref
description: Linus review of Stage 3 unlock flagged that RETURN -n.num parses as a RefProjection to n.num rather than residual ErrUnsupportedProjection. Cause: the shared precedence-tower collapse (shape.go nonArithmeticFromAddSub) reads OC_UnaryAddOrSubtractExpression(0) without rejecting a leading '-'. Pre-existing behaviour governing Stage 0-2 too, not introduced by Stage 3, and no TCK read-core scenario exercises it — hence non-blocking. Decide whether a unary sign is arithmetic that should be residual (value lives below the type-interface boundary either way, ADR 0005) and tighten the helper if so.
close_reason: PR #36 merged: unary-signed operands now residual via GetChildCount guard in nonArithmeticFromAddSub
